definitions.text: comparative degree of many: in greater number.
examples: There are more ways to do this than I can count.

definitions.text: comparative degree of much: in greater quantity, amount, or proportion.
examples: There's more caffeine in my coffee than in the coffee you get in most places.

definitions.text: To a greater degree or extent. .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller}
examples: I like cake, but I like chocolate more.
I could no more climb that than fly!

definitions.text: Used to form the comparative form of adjectives and adverbs.
examples: You're a great deal more beautiful than I ever imagined.
I am much more eager to help than you.

definitions.text: A greater number or quantity (of something).
examples: We’re running out of napkins. I should have bought more.
There isn’t enough salt in this. You need to add more.

definitions.text: An extra or additional quantity (of something).
examples: There aren’t many people here yet, but more should be arriving soon.

definitions.text: comparative degree of many: in greater number.
examples: Last year’s applications received from new and returning students were more than each of the previous four years.

definitions.text: comparative degree of much: in greater quantity, amount, or proportion.
